text: When Mei moved into her new flat, she could barely fit a bed and a desk in the same room.
The interior felt dark and cramped.
Her first impulse was to buy more furniture, but she quickly realised that would only make things worse.
Instead, she decided to eliminate everything she did not use.
Old clothes, spare cables, books she would never read again — all of it went into boxes for charity.
She found subtle ways to create space: a mirror on the wall made the room look wider, and a tall shelf replaced the stiff old bookcase that had blocked the window.
Within a week, her surplus of clutter had vanished.
The flat was still small, but it finally felt like home.
Mei learned that sometimes you gain more by removing things than by adding them.
A squeeze on space can actually push you toward smarter choices.
